topTargets.name: Carnell Tate
topTargets.reasoning: RSP's only Franchise-tier grade in the class (DoT 92.3, Moss comp) and ADP 9.46 says he is the consensus 1.02 if Love goes 1.01. WR-thin roster (6 WRs vs 8 RBs) makes this a need-and-BPA fit; $12.9M cap space absorbs the rookie hit comfortably without taxi.

topTargets.name: Jeremiyah Love
topTargets.reasoning: If Fire Ready Aim breaks ADP and takes Tate at 1.01, Love (RSP #2, DoT 89.3, All-Pro ceiling per Waldman, Kamara comp) is the obvious consolation at 1.02. ADP 3.59 means he is the truer consensus 1.01 and almost certainly off the board, but DEAD happily takes him if he falls.

topTargets.name: Makai Lemon
topTargets.reasoning: Pure fallback contingency at 1.02 if both Tate and Love are gone (very unlikely with only one pick ahead). RSP #3 (DoT 88.8, Amon-Ra comp) and ADP 11.18 makes him the next-best WR available and addresses the same WR need.

topTargets.name: Antonio Williams
topTargets.reasoning: Realistic 3.14 target — RSP #18 (DoT 83.9) with Jayden Reed comp, ADP 29.06 puts him squarely in the third-round range. Medium-RSP-affinity GM blending boards loves the value gap between ADP and Waldman's grade here, and stacks the WR room further.

topTargets.name: Chris Bell
topTargets.reasoning: Alternate 3.14 dart — RSP #16 (DoT 83.9) with A.J. Brown high-end comp, ADP 28.24. Waldman explicitly says 'wait for second round if falls,' which is exactly what a 3.14 represents. Same WR-need logic.

capPosture: Healthy — $12.9M of $45M open, easily absorbs a 1.02 rookie on the active roster with no taxi gymnastics required.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: Only 2 TEs on the roster and RSP #11 (DoT 84.4, on-cusp Starter, classic move TE) with ADP 21.25 means he could slip into 3.14 range if WRs run early. Medium-RSP-affinity GM might break consensus to grab a positional scarcity play.

summary: Dead Cap Walking sits at 1.02 with cap flexibility and a clear WR need, so the play is simple: take whichever of Tate/Love survives Fire Ready Aim's 1.01 — almost certainly Tate given Love's much stronger ADP. At 3.14, target a WR with RSP-board value above ADP (Antonio Williams or Chris Bell) to keep stacking the thin pass-catcher room. No need to play taxi games — the cap absorbs the rookie hit cleanly.
